notes: Security is weak: no authentication, no origin validation on WS upgrade, no nickname uniqueness enforcement (impersonation possible), no message sanitization, user-controlled type reflected in error response, zero rate limiting. Error handling is decent with try/catch on JSON parse, readyState checks before send, null guards, and ws error handler, but ws.send() calls outside the main try/catch could throw on closed sockets, and binary frames are not rejected before toString(). Best practices are reasonable with extracted constants, validation helpers, Maps for lookups, health endpoint, graceful shutdown, and module exports, but has code duplication (leave-room logic duplicated in join and close handlers, typing broadcast duplicated, error responses repeated), UUID_REGEX defined but never used (dead code), and variable shadowing of 'room'. Robustness has good input validation (regex, length bounds), bounded message history, empty room cleanup, and typing auto-timeout, but critically lacks rate limiting, connection limits, room count limits, ping/pong heartbeat for dead connections, and max WebSocket frame size configuration.
